description_long: This stone church features a rectangular nave with a pitched, tiled roof and a prominent cylindrical bell tower attached to one side. The exterior walls are constructed from rough-hewn stone blocks, with vertical pilasters and arched window recesses adding architectural detail. The bell tower is capped with a polygonal roof and open arches, suggesting a space for a bell. The main facade includes a small arched doorway and several narrow windows, while the rear of the building is semi-circular with a cross relief above the apse. The roof is covered in reddish-brown tiles, and the structure is accented by a series of blind arcades along the upper walls. The overall design is solid and imposing, with minimal ornamentation and a focus on structural mass.
description: A stone church with a tiled roof, cylindrical bell tower, arched windows, and a cross relief above the apse. The structure is robust, with rough stone walls and minimal decorative elements.
description_view_0: Front and side view showing the cylindrical bell tower, tiled roof, and arched window recesses.
description_view_1: Side and partial rear view highlighting the length of the nave and the polygonal roof of the bell tower.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the main entrance door, pilasters, and a row of blind arcades along the upper wall.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the semi-circular apse with a cross relief and the bell tower in the background.
